source_paper_title: Online Estimation and Optimization of Utility-Based Shortfall Risk

Page 33, Section 9 (Concluding Remarks and Future Work).

Third, it remains open to extend our finite sample bounds to the estimator of multivariate shortfall risk measure (MSRM) [2]. In a recent work [41], the authors derive asymptotic consistency and normality results for an estimator of MSRM, while finite sample bounds are not available.
title: Finite-sample bounds for multivariate shortfall risk estimators with streaming samples
background_latex:
Let \((\Omega,\mathcal F,\mathbb P)\) be a probability space. Fix an integer dimension \(d\ge 2\). Let \(X\in L^\infty(\Omega;\mathbb R^d)\) be a bounded \(d\)-dimensional random vector representing (componentwise) losses or positions.

Let \(\ell:\mathbb R^d\to\mathbb R\) be a convex loss/utility-penalty function (sometimes called an aggregation loss), and let \(\lambda\in\mathbb R\) be a fixed risk-level parameter (assumed to be in the interior of the range of \(\ell\) under the law of the argument in question).

Define the acceptance set
\[
\mathcal A := \bigl\{Y\in L^\infty(\Omega;\mathbb R^d): \ \mathbb E[\,\ell(-Y)\,] \le \lambda\bigr\}.
\]

A multivariate shortfall risk measure (MSRM) is a multivariate analogue of utility-based shortfall risk, defined via the cash-additivity/translation of the acceptance set: for a given position \(X\), one seeks a deterministic capital allocation vector \(m\in\mathbb R^d\) such that \(X+m\in\mathcal A\); one may consider the set of admissible allocations
\[
\mathcal R_\lambda(X) := \{m\in\mathbb R^d : \mathbb E[\ell(-(X+m))]\le \lambda\},
\]
and/or a scalarized risk value derived from \(\mathcal R_\lambda(X)\) (the precise scalarization depends on the MSRM definition).

Assume an online/streaming observation model: samples \(\{X_i\}_{i\ge 1}\) are revealed one-at-a-time, where \(X_i\) are i.i.d. copies of \(X\). An estimator for an MSRM is any measurable mapping \(\widehat{\mathcal R}_{\lambda,n}\) (or \(\widehat m_n\)) computed from \(X_1,\dots,X_n\). “Finite-sample bounds” refers to non-asymptotic guarantees (e.g., mean-squared error or concentration inequalities) as explicit functions of \(n\).
problem_definition_latex:
Develop non-asymptotic (finite-sample) performance guarantees for an online/streaming estimator of a multivariate shortfall risk measure (MSRM) based on i.i.d. samples \(X_1,\dots,X_n\) of a bounded random vector \(X\in\mathbb R^d\).

Concretely, provide explicit bounds (in \(n\)) on the estimation error of an MSRM estimator \(\widehat{\mathcal R}_{\lambda,n}\) (or an estimated capital allocation \(\widehat m_n\in\mathbb R^d\)) relative to the target MSRM determined by \(\ell\) and \(\lambda\), analogous in spirit to finite-sample bounds available for the univariate utility-based shortfall risk estimator.

Let \(\ell:\mathbb R^d\to\mathbb R\) be a convex loss/utility-penalty function (sometimes called an aggregation loss), and let \(\lambda\in\mathbb R\) be a fixed risk-level parameter (assumed to be in the interior of the range of \(\ell\) under the law of the argument in question).

\[

Develop non-asymptotic (finite-sample) performance guarantees for an online/streaming estimator of a multivariate shortfall risk measure (MSRM) based on i.i.d. samples \(X_1,\dots,X_n\) of a bounded random vector \(X\in\mathbb R^d\).

location_in_paper: Page 33, Section 9 (Concluding Remarks and Future Work).
open_problem_quote: Third, it remains open to extend our finite sample bounds to the estimator of multivariate shortfall risk measure (MSRM) [2]. In a recent work [41], the authors derive asymptotic consistency and normality results for an estimator of MSRM, while finite sample bounds are not available.
counterexample_suitability_rationale: The problem asks for universal upper bounds (rates and constants) rather than existence of a counterexample. Automated search over instances is unlikely to falsify the statement, since the missing object is a proof technique and a bound that should hold uniformly over distributions satisfying assumptions.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: One can represent candidate MSRM estimators/recursions (e.g., projected stochastic approximation schemes in \(\mathbb R^d\)) and evaluate them empirically on simulated distributions with a computable proxy loss (empirical risk of constraint violation or distance to a high-accuracy offline solution). However, translating empirical performance into provable finite-sample bounds would still require substantial human proof work.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.

literature_review.synthesis:
The source paper (Hegde–Menon–Prashanth–Jagannathan, 2023) establishes finite-sample (non-asymptotic) bounds for online estimation of the univariate utility-based shortfall risk \(\mathrm{SR}_\lambda(X)\) by casting estimation as stochastic root finding for \(g(t)=\mathbb E[\ell(-X-t)]-\lambda\) and analyzing a projected Robbins–Monro recursion. The open problem asks for an analogous non-asymptotic theory in the multivariate shortfall risk measure (MSRM) setting, where one estimates a capital allocation vector \(m\in\mathbb R^d\) such that \(\mathbb E[\ell(-(X+m))]\le \lambda\), i.e., the feasible set \(\mathcal R_\lambda(X)\). The foundational MSRM object and its convex-analytic properties are developed in “Multivariate shortfall risk allocation and systemic risk”, which formalizes the acceptance set \(\{Y: \mathbb E[\ell(-Y)]\le \lambda\}\) and the associated allocation/risk allocation viewpoint.

On the estimation side, the closest direct progress toward MSRM estimation is “Estimation of systemic shortfall risk measure using stochastic algorithms”, which provides stochastic-algorithmic estimators and proves asymptotic consistency and asymptotic normality. However, it does not deliver explicit finite-sample error bounds as functions of \(n\) in the streaming i.i.d. model. Bridging this gap likely requires importing non-asymptotic stochastic approximation tools (e.g., “Concentration Bounds for Stochastic Approximations” and non-asymptotic SA/SGD analyses such as Moulines–Bach) but in a genuinely multivariate setting: MSRM estimation naturally leads to vector-valued monotone inclusions/KKT systems (e.g., via scalarizations or Lagrange multipliers for selecting a unique allocation), rather than a single scalar root. Moreover, if one estimates a particular allocation rule (e.g., minimal total capital \(\min 1^\top m\) subject to \(\mathbb E[\ell(-(X+m))]\le\lambda\)), the resulting online algorithm resembles stochastic primal–dual or biased-gradient schemes, for which batching/bias-control techniques (as in work on biased noisy gradient oracles) may be essential.

Overall, the problem remains open: existing MSRM work provides definitions, duality, and asymptotic estimation theory, while the univariate UBSR literature provides sharp \(O(1/n)\) or \(O(1/n^\alpha)\) finite-sample bounds for streaming SA. A promising direction is to (i) choose a canonical scalarization/selection of \(m\) (to avoid set-valued targets), (ii) express the target as the solution of a strongly monotone variational inequality or saddle-point problem under assumptions on \(\ell\) (e.g., smoothness and uniform strong convexity in \(m\)), and (iii) apply modern non-asymptotic analyses for stochastic monotone operator splitting/primal–dual SA to obtain explicit MSE and high-probability bounds in \(n\), with dimension dependence tracked explicitly.

sources.title: Multivariate shortfall risk allocation and systemic risk
sources.theorem_or_result: Introduces and studies multivariate shortfall risk measures and associated capital allocations via acceptance sets of the form \(\{Y: \mathbb E[\ell(-Y)]\le \lambda\}\), including existence/uniqueness and dual representations under convex-analytic conditions on \(\ell\).
sources.connection: Provides the precise MSRM object \(\mathcal R_\lambda(X)\) and systemic-risk interpretation that the open problem asks to estimate; however it does not give non-asymptotic, streaming finite-sample bounds.

sources.title: Estimation of systemic shortfall risk measure using stochastic algorithms
sources.theorem_or_result: Develops stochastic-algorithmic estimators for systemic/multivariate shortfall risk and proves asymptotic consistency and asymptotic normality (CLT-type) results for the estimator under regularity conditions.
sources.connection: Directly targets MSRM estimation but (as noted in the source paper) focuses on asymptotics; it is the closest partial progress toward the requested finite-sample streaming bounds.

sources.title: Stochastic root finding and efficient estimation of convex risk measures
sources.theorem_or_result: Studies stochastic approximation/root-finding schemes for estimating convex risk measures (including shortfall-type) and provides asymptotic convergence and efficiency results under convexity/monotonicity assumptions.
sources.connection: Supplies foundational SA methodology and conditions (monotonicity of \(g\), projection) that can be adapted to multivariate shortfall constraints; does not provide the desired non-asymptotic multivariate bounds.

sources.title: Utility-based shortfall risk: Efficient computations via Monte Carlo
sources.theorem_or_result: Provides Monte Carlo/sample-average approximation algorithms for UBSR, including sensitivity/derivative formulas used for optimization, with asymptotic guarantees and practical computation schemes.
sources.connection: The univariate streaming finite-sample analysis in the source paper builds on this computational viewpoint; for MSRM, analogous SAA estimators exist but finite-sample streaming error bounds remain to be developed.

sources.title: Concentration Bounds for Stochastic Approximations
sources.theorem_or_result: Derives non-asymptotic concentration inequalities for stochastic approximation schemes under contractivity/monotonicity-type conditions, yielding high-probability bounds for SA iterates.
sources.connection: Provides a general finite-sample SA concentration toolkit that could be extended from the scalar root \(g(t)=0\) in UBSR to vector-valued roots/KKT systems arising in MSRM capital allocation estimation.

sources.title: Non-asymptotic analysis of stochastic approximation algorithms for machine learning
sources.theorem_or_result: Provides non-asymptotic mean-square error bounds for stochastic approximation/SGD under strong convexity/monotonicity and smoothness assumptions, including last-iterate behavior.
sources.connection: The source paper’s univariate \(O(1/n)\) MSE bounds mirror this framework; extending to MSRM likely requires adapting these SA techniques to vector-valued monotone operators and constraint qualifications.

sources.title: Non-asymptotic bounds for stochastic optimization with biased noisy gradient oracles
sources.theorem_or_result: Develops non-asymptotic convergence bounds for stochastic optimization when gradient oracles are biased but bias can be reduced via batching, including step-size/batch-size tradeoffs.
sources.connection: MSRM estimation/optimization in streaming settings can lead to biased gradient/dual-variable updates (e.g., when estimating \(\mathbb E[\ell(-X-m)]\) and its subgradients); this provides a template for finite-sample bounds with batching.
